question1.description:
An event-driven application processes thousands of actions simultaneously. However, developers are finding it difficult to trace the origin of errors and understand the complete flow of operations.
Which quality attribute is being most compromised?

question2.description:
Educational software uses a Microkernel architecture. New features can be added as plug-ins without changing the main core of the system.
Which alternative best explains the main advantage of this model?

choices.correct: Ease of extension and system adaptation

question3.description:
A company developed a hybrid system: the main core uses Microkernel; notifications use an event-driven architecture; and extra modules are installed as plug-ins.
During testing, it was noticed that failures in a plug-in did not bring down the core, but lost events caused temporary inconsistencies.
Which alternative presents the MOST appropriate analysis?

choices.correct: The Microkernel improves fault isolation, but asynchronous events can reduce reliability
choices.wrong1: The system has high reliability in all components
